title: Walk-Forward HMM Retraining Pipeline — Preventing Model Staleness in Live Trading
summary: Implement a scheduled monthly job that retrains the Gaussian HMM on a rolling 5-year window of SPY log-returns and automatically validates the new model against the last 90 days of held-out data before replacing the production model. This ensures the regime classifier stays calibrated to current market structure. The QuantInsti walk-forward backtesting research confirms that regime-adaptive models must remain adaptive over time — a model trained once will degrade as market structure evolves. The validation gate (compare IC of old vs new model on held-out data) prevents a degraded model from going live.
expected_impact: Prevents out-of-sample regime model decay; the QuantStart HMM study explicitly warns that models trained on 1993-2004 data and applied to 2005-2014 without retraining still worked — but that was a static market structure period; post-2020 regime shifts (COVID, rate cycle) require periodic retraining
